name: Ruta de los Pilones – Natural reserve of the Garganta de los Infiernos (Hell's Gorge)
name_original: Ruta de los Pilones — Reserva natural de la Garganta de los Infiernos
description:
Cáceres is a hiker's paradise and, if you don't believe it, you just have to go to the Hells Gorge to see it. 

The Route starts from an interpretation center with very valuable information about the environment and ascends among oaks until you reach the Pilones, where you can take a refreshing bath and recharge with water at the fountain. Afterwards, you will have the necessary strength to discover the incredible Hells Gorge and return to the same place where this circular route begins.

Any time is good to do this tour. To get to the starting point, it is best that you have your own vehicle. If you don't have one, you can also start the Route in the town of Jerte, which has a bus station.
region_intro: Hiking around Jerte offers diverse trails through the Gredos massif in Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by the Jerte River, which carves gorges, waterfalls, and natural pools, notably within the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve. The landscape features extensive cherry orchards, oak groves, and high mountain areas, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Jerte The most popular hiking route is The Pilones of the Garganta de los Infiernos (Gorge of the Hells), a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete. This moderate path leads to the iconic natural rock pools formed by water erosion. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ruta de los Pilones – Natural reserve of the Garganta de los Infiernos (Hell's Gorge), a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. It offers a longer exploration of the reserve's unique rock formations and riverine landscapes. Local hikers also love the Los Pilones Natural Pools – Carlos V Bridge loop from Parking gratuito "El Gorrilla", a 10.0 miles (16.0 km) trail leading through forests and past historical points, often completed in about 5 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Jerte is defined by crystal-clear rivers, deep gorges, and lush deciduous forests, with trails ranging from easy walks to more challenging ascents into the high mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from family-friendly routes to demanding treks. The routes in Jerte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Jerte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the different difficulty levels for hiking trails in Jerte Valley?
A: Jerte Valley offers a diverse range of hiking trails suitable for various experience levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 9 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both casual walkers and experienced trekkers can find a suitable adventure.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Jerte Valley?
A: Yes, Jerte Valley has options for families. Shorter, easier walks, particularly those leading to the iconic natural pools of Los Pilones within the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve, are often suitable for families. These trails allow you to experience the valley's unique rock formations and refreshing waters.

Q: Where can I find waterfalls and natural pools on hikes in Jerte Valley?
A: The Jerte Valley is renowned for its stunning water features. The most famous spot is &lt;strong&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/strong&gt; within the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve, where water erosion has sculpted spectacular giant rock pools perfect for a dip. You can explore this area via routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2359868754/the-pilones-of-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos-gorge-of-the-hells" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Pilones of the Garganta de los Infiernos&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive Chorrero de la Virgen.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Jerte Valley?
A: Yes, several circular routes allow you to start and end at the same point, offering convenient exploration. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17315128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools – Carlos V Bridge loop from Parking gratuito "El Gorrilla"&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 16 km trail. Another moderate loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6910581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte River – Los Pilones Natural Pools loop from Parking gratuito "El Gorrilla"&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available near the main hiking trailheads in Jerte Valley?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near popular trailheads. For instance, routes leading to Los Pilones often have designated parking areas, such as the 'Parking gratuito "El Gorrilla"', which serves as a starting point for several loops.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Jerte Valley?
A: The Jerte Valley offers unique beauty throughout the year. Spring (March-April) is spectacular during the cherry blossom season, when the valley transforms into a 'sea of white petals'. Autumn (November) is equally stunning with an 'ocean of golden leaves'. Summer provides opportunities for refreshing dips in natural pools, while winter offers a different, serene landscape, especially in higher elevations.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see along the trails?
A: The Jerte Valley is rich in natural landmarks and scenic viewpoints. Beyond the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;, you can find impressive summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2671693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón (Calvitero Summit)&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the Gredos massif. Various 'miradores' (viewpoints) like Mirador del Valle del Jerte and Mirador de la Memoria provide breathtaking vistas of the entire valley and its changing seasonal colors.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking options in Jerte Valley?
A: While popular spots like Los Pilones can attract visitors, the Jerte Valley offers many trails. To find less crowded options, consider exploring routes that venture into the high mountain areas, such as those accessible via the Peña Negra Route, or lesser-known trails around villages like Cabezuela del Valle, Jerte, and Tornavacas. These often provide a more tranquil experience amidst oak and holm oak groves.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Jerte Valley?
A: The Jerte Valley is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural landscapes, particularly the crystal-clear rivers, deep gorges, and abundant waterfalls. The unique rock formations of Los Pilones and the seasonal beauty of the cherry orchards are also often highlighted as favorite aspects.

Q: Can I access hiking trails in Jerte Valley using public transport?
A: While specific public transport options directly to every trailhead might be limited, the picturesque villages within the Jerte Valley, such as Jerte, Cabezuela del Valle, and Tornavacas, are generally accessible by regional bus services. From these villages, you can often find trails starting directly or within a short walk, allowing for car-free exploration of some areas.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a longer, more immersive experience of the Garganta de los Infiernos?
A: Yes, for a more extensive exploration of the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497795617/ruta-de-los-pilones-natural-reserve-of-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos-hell-s-gorge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Pilones – Natural reserve of the Garganta de los Infiernos (Hell's Gorge)&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 13.3 km path offers a deeper dive into the reserve's unique rock formations and riverine landscapes, taking approximately 4 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any historical trails in the Jerte Valley?
A: Yes, the Jerte Valley is home to the historic Carlos V Trail. This more challenging linear route, approximately 27.7 kilometers long, follows the path Emperor Charles V took on his final journey. It features medieval bridges and ancient oak forests, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical significance.
tips: This impressive natural setting is one of those that remain stuck in the retina. With no more and no less than 13 natural pools, this incredible ravine shows off curious rock formations, the result of the action of water. It could be said that it is the heart of the Garganta de los Infiernos nature reserve.

This beautiful stone bridge, which crosses over the Tres Cerros stream, is located in a spectacular setting. It is a much less crowded area than Los Pilones, and is part of the Carlos V hiking route. There are some pools of transparent water in which to relax your feet before continuing the march.

This beautiful viewpoint has a small booth, benches and various information panels. Although in the rainy season it is easier to see, on clear days you can see the Manto de la Virgen waterfall in the distance. It is located less than a kilometer from Los Pilones.
